title: File Permissions and Ownership
description: Understand and change file permissions with chmod.

slides.title: File Permissions
slides.content:
# Understanding Permissions

On Unix-like systems, files have permissions for three categories: owner, group, and others. Each has read (r), write (w), and execute (x) permissions.

Permissions are often represented as an octal number (e.g., 0644).

*   `0644` – owner: read+write (6), group: read (4), others: read (4)
*   `0755` – owner: read+write+execute (7), group: read+execute (5), others: read+execute (5)

slides.title: chmod() – Change Permissions
slides.content:
# chmod()

`chmod(string $filename, int $permissions): bool` changes file permissions. The permissions must be an octal number, usually with a leading zero.

```php
chmod('file.txt', 0644); // make file readable by all, writable by owner
chmod('script.sh', 0755); // make executable
```

Returns true on success, false on failure.

slides.title: chmod Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$file = 'config.php';
if (chmod($file, 0644)) {
    echo "Permissions changed.";
} else {
    echo "Failed to change permissions.";
}
?>

slides.title: fileperms() – Get Permissions
slides.content:
# fileperms()

`fileperms(string $filename): int|false` returns the permissions as a numeric mode. You can format it with `sprintf('%o', fileperms($file))` to get octal representation.

```php
$perms = fileperms('file.txt');
echo substr(sprintf('%o', $perms), -4); // e.g., 0644
```

slides.title: Getting and Setting Permissions
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$file = 'data.txt';
if (file_exists($file)) {
    $perms = fileperms($file);
    echo "Current permissions: " . substr(sprintf('%o', $perms), -4) . "\n";
    chmod($file, 0644);
    echo "Permissions updated.\n";
} else {
    echo "File not found.";
}
?>

slides.title: Security Note
slides.content:
## Avoid 0777

Never set permissions to 0777 (world writable) on a production server unless absolutely necessary. It can allow any user on the system to modify your files, leading to security risks.

quizData.question: What does the octal permission 0644 mean?

options.text: Owner can read/write, group and others can read
Owner can read/write/execute, others can read
Owner can read, group can write, others can execute

quizData.explanation: 6 = read+write (4+2), 4 = read, so 0644 means owner read/write, group read, others read.
